assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: The response specifically mentions testing on dev clusters (Rapid channel) that are already running 1.32+ before the prod upgrade, and tailors recommendations accordingly

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: The response includes an 'Autopilot-Specific Considerations' section that mentions no control over upgrade timing, node pools managed by Google, limited rollback options, and resource limits/requirements

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: The response provides a specific kubectl command for checking deprecated API usage: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_requested_deprecated_apis'

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: The Autopilot-Specific Considerations section explicitly mentions 'ensure your workloads comply with Autopilot resource requirements'

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: While the checklist includes general workload testing and compatibility assessment, it does not specifically mention PodDisruptionBudgets, bare pods, or graceful shutdown configurations

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: The response includes specific kubectl commands for verifying cluster health and versions: 'kubectl get nodes', 'kubectl get componentstatuses'

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: The post-upgrade checklist includes comprehensive workload health checks including pod status, application endpoints, service discovery, and persistent volumes

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: The pre-upgrade checklist specifically includes 'Verify your dev clusters (Rapid channel) are already running 1.32+ successfully' and 'Run full application test suite on dev clusters'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: The response mentions that prod clusters will auto-upgrade 'next month' but does not specifically discuss the GKE release schedule or the ~1 month advance notice timing

The response includes extensive monitoring items such as 'Validate monitoring, logging, and alerting work correctly', 'Review cluster metrics and performance baselines', and dedicated monitoring sections in both pre and post-upgrade checklists
